# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Pull Request Workflow

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, edited, reopened]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Get PR title and description
        id: pr_info
        run: |
          echo "PR_TITLE=$(echo '${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}')" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "PR_BODY=$(echo '${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}')" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Commit changes with PR title
        run: |
          git config --local user.email "action@github.com"
          git config --local user.name "GitHub Action"
          git commit -m "Deploying feature: ${{ env.PR_TITLE }}" || echo "No changes to commit"

      - name: Notify build status
        run: |
          curl -X POST -H "Authorization: token ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_TOKEN }}" \
          -d '{"title": "${{ env.PR_TITLE }}", "body": "${{ env.PR_BODY }}", "status": "success"}' \
          https://api.notification.service/send

      - name: Cleanup
        run: git clean -fdx
